_meta.addendum_note: A shared addendum was issued 2026-04-15 carrying status updates relevant to all 4 remaining branches: Grok branch complete (Novel for Penrose); canonical HUF-GOV definition locked in; shape/magnitude decomposition as organizing principle; Master Lineage established; Coimbra welcome package in preparation. Read the addendum before executing this briefing. Core task unchanged; interpretation context has deepened.

context.background: The Higgins Unity Framework (HUF) develops the Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer (EITT): Shannon entropy of compositional data is near-invariant under geometric-mean block decimation across temporal resolutions. Drift events — points where autocorrelation breaks and a new regime takes over — behave like interfaces in time. If these interfaces have universal fluctuation scaling, they may live in the KPZ (Kardar-Parisi-Zhang) universality class.
context.recent_synthesis: A shape/magnitude decomposition generalizes EITT: shape functionals are invariant under decimation, magnitude functionals scale as 1/M. KPZ universality governs interface dynamics in many systems; if compositional drift interfaces show KPZ scaling exponents (1/3 and 2/3), that would be a deep connection.
context.novelty_claim_under_test: A five-object cluster (EITT + Tutte + Hanani-Tutte + Penrose + KPZ) is novel. Your branch tests the KPZ side: has anyone connected KPZ universality, interface growth, or statistical-physics scaling theory to compositional data or simplex-valued time series?

your_branch_assignment.scope: KPZ universality, interface growth models, random matrix theory, directed polymers, stochastic growth models, Wasserstein gradient flows on the simplex, information geometry of stochastic processes — and their connections to compositional data.
your_branch_assignment.why_gemini: Strong on multi-modal technical search and good coverage of physics/math journals. KPZ universality has massive literature across statistical physics, probability, and mathematical physics venues — wide-net search with physics sensibility is needed.

hypothesis_to_test.H1: No paper has explicitly connected KPZ universality to compositional data analysis or simplex-valued time series.
hypothesis_to_test.H2: Random matrix theory connections to compositional data exist at a statistical level but have not been framed as universality class membership.
hypothesis_to_test.H3: Wasserstein gradient flows of entropy on the simplex exist as pure-math objects (Villani, Otto, Ambrosio) but have not been connected to compositional time series practice.
hypothesis_to_test.H4: Phase-field methods on the simplex (multi-component materials science) exist but have not been adapted to compositional monitoring or EITT-style coarse-graining.

tasks.action: Filter for probability/stat-mech simplex (not geometric simplices of graphs). Report top 10 relevant.
Direct test. Any hit is significant.
Tests whether CoDa has ever been framed as a universality problem.
Probability simplex is the natural KPZ setting — any crossover paper is notable.
Tests H2. Report any paper treating compositional covariance as a random-matrix object.
This query will return many results. The question is whether any of them connect to compositional data practice (not pure math). Filter for data-analysis applications.
Tests H4. Phase-field methods on simplex exist in materials science (Moelans, Folch-Plapp) but the link to statistical CoDa is what we want.
Tests whether the KPZ scaling exponent has been measured in any applied time series context beyond physics.
Directed polymers are a KPZ-class model. Probe for simplex-based formulations.

tasks.target: Recent review papers on KPZ universality (e.g., Corwin 2012 'The Kardar-Parisi-Zhang equation and universality class' and updates)
tasks.action: Scan for mentions of probability simplices, compositional data, Aitchison geometry, log-ratio coordinates, or any statistical-methodology connection. Most likely none — but confirm.
Based on T1-T10, assess hypotheses H1-H4 with confidence levels. KPZ is this branch's weakest novelty claim (largest literature on both sides). Give a candid judgment.

required_fields.task_results: array of {task_id, query, n_hits, top_hits: [{title, authors, year, venue, relevance}], verdict}
required_fields.review_paper_findings: notes on whether KPZ review papers mention compositional/simplex applications
required_fields.strong_candidates_for_positive_hit: List any specific paper that could preempt the KPZ-CoDa connection

verdict_definitions.positive: A paper explicitly applies KPZ universality, random matrix theory, or interface-growth theory to compositional data or simplex-valued time series. Flag for escalation.
verdict_definitions.near_miss: A paper uses Wasserstein gradient flows or phase-field methods on the simplex in a mathematical context without connecting to CoDa practice. Valuable reference but does not negate novelty.
verdict_definitions.negative: No positive hits. The KPZ-CoDa connection remains novel.

overlap_with_others.Grok: Grok is checking Penrose / aperiodic order. Arctic circle theorem and random-tiling limit shapes are KPZ-adjacent — if Grok finds statistical-physics tiling results connected to compositional data, reconcile with your T4.
overlap_with_others.Copilot: Copilot is scanning arXiv. math-ph + stat.ME crossover papers are most likely to contain KPZ-CoDa attempts; if Copilot finds any, cross-check with your results.

coordination_notes.escalate_if: Any T1-T9 returns a direct positive hit, or T10 finds the CoDa/simplex connection mentioned in a KPZ review.

honest_notes.this_is_the_weakest_novelty_branch: KPZ universality has been connected to many things and the probability-simplex setting is natural enough that it is the most likely branch to have been touched somewhere. Don't be afraid to report a positive finding if you find one — the framework benefits more from correct citation than from maintaining a novelty claim.
honest_notes.scope_creep_warning: Pure-math results on Wasserstein flow on the simplex (Otto, Ambrosio, Villani, Carrillo) are legitimate near-misses. Do NOT classify them as positive hits unless they explicitly connect to compositional data practice (not just simplex as an abstract object).

estimated_effort: 2-3 hours. KPZ literature is extensive and requires filtering. Take the time.
